metric_value: 10 to 800 managed nodes (site and workgroup level)
notes: Above 800 nodes, DEC offered POLYCENTER Manager on NetView

metric_value: Open partner platform: DEC provides base functionality; partners provide domain-specific applications
notes: Contrasted with closed, tightly integrated approach of McAfee and Symantec

metric_value: NetWare 3.x/4.x, LAN Manager 2.x, LAN Server, PATHWORKS, partial Windows NT
notes: Windows NT management was partial; full NT support was a gap

metric_value: Windows 3.1, Windows for Workgroups, Windows 95, Windows NT on Intel and Alpha
notes: Cross-platform Windows console was a differentiator in 1996

metric_value: ManageWORKS forwards SNMP traps to POLYCENTER, NetView for AIX, HP OpenView, or any SNMP-capable enterprise manager
notes: Mid-level manager architecture localizes polling and passes only relevant events upstream

metric_value: Event Manager acts as processor/repository; pollers distribute across network; console provides UI
notes: Distributed polling architecture was ahead of its time for LAN management

metric_value: Allows plugging any DOS/Windows application into ManageWORKS without writing code
notes: Low-code extensibility feature; SDK also available for tighter 32-bit integration

metric_value: OEM licensing allows PC server and comms device vendors to use ManageWORKS as vendor-specific management base
notes: OEM strategy extended ManageWORKS reach beyond DEC's own customer base

metric_value: Configuration management, hardware/software inventory, software distribution, metering, remote control, virus protection
notes: LANDesk later became standalone product; now Ivanti Endpoint Manager

metric_value: Tightly integrated proprietary product suite; not designed for third-party integration
notes: Aberdeen positioned this as inferior to ManageWORKS open approach for best-of-breed deployments

metric_value: ManageWORKS open integration approach is unique in the LAN management market; appeals to best-of-breed builders
notes: Aberdeen gave high marks to platform openness; actual market adoption was limited by DEC's overall market position

metric_value: Open platform strategy positions ManageWORKS to maintain competitive edge in LAN management market
notes: Implied prediction of continued market relevance via partner ecosystem strategy

metric_name: ManageWORKS strategic advantage viability - actual outcome
metric_value: Digital Equipment Corporation acquired by Compaq in June 1998; ManageWORKS product line discontinued
notes: DEC's acquisition by Compaq for $9.6B ended ManageWORKS development; open platform strategy was moot after acquisition

metric_value: POLYCENTER Manager on NetView positioned as higher-end SNMP platform for large installations beyond ManageWORKS scope
notes: Two-tier architecture: ManageWORKS at workgroup/site, POLYCENTER at enterprise

metric_value: Performance, accounting, software asset/configuration, network, security, storage, problem, automation management (via partners)
notes: Core ManageWORKS provided discovery, event monitoring, print management, user/group management; partner apps added depth

metric_value: User interface for event/network status viewing and monitoring parameter configuration
notes: Component 1 of 3 in ManageWORKS architecture

metric_value: Central processor and repository; logs all alerts; forwards SNMP traps to enterprise managers
notes: Component 2 of 3; typically one Event Manager per Console

metric_value: Distributed SNMP pollers tasked with collecting status from specified nodes/subnetworks; communicate with Event Manager via TCP/IP socket
notes: Component 3 of 3; distributes polling responsibility to minimize SNMP traffic

metric_name: Aberdeen assessment of enterprise management role
metric_value: ManageWORKS gives users a consistent view of disparate networks; no other product offers this
notes: Aberdeen's strong claim of uniqueness in cross-NOS visibility; overstated given emerging competition from CA, IBM

metric_value: Multiple consoles can access same Event Manager data simultaneously (read-only); single console can update; data stored on separate file server
notes: Shared management data architecture enabled multi-admin scenarios
